rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Further Deposition Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel further deposition of the person most knowledgeable and custodian of records of defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and request for sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Interrogatories From Groeniger & Company
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories from Groeniger & Co. is continued to April 19, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 503 per the April 11 stipulated order. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Responses To General Order 129 Standard Interrogatories From Soco West, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, April 12, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel responses to General Order 129 standard plaintiff interrogatories from Soco West, Inc. is granted. General Order 129 requires that defendant provide such responses. Defendant must provide its responses within 14 days of the date of this hearing. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions in the amount of $1061.00 is granted. Plaintiffs¿ request that hearings on any dispositive motions filed by defendant in asbestos cases before this court be stayed until 60 days after defendant has provided verified responses is granted. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
